topics: Principles of Drawing: Free Hand Sketching, Manual Drawing, CAD Drawing
Code of Practice for Engineering Drawing: BIS Specifications, Lines and Types of Lines, Dimensioning, Sectional Views, Welding Symbols, Riveted Joints, Keys, Fasteners (Bolts, Nuts, Screws)
Limits, Fits, and Tolerances: Tolerances of Individual Dimensions, Specification of Fits, Geometric and Dimensional Tolerances, Surface Roughness and Indication
Preparation of Production Drawings and Reading of Part and Assembly Drawings, Exercises on Fasteners
Couplings: Oldham's Coupling, Flexible Couplings, Universal Joints
Introduction to CAD: Input and Output Devices, Drafting Software (AutoCAD), Basic Commands, Development of Simple 2D and 3D Drawings, Different File Formats for 3D Modelling (IGES, STL)
CAD Operations: Drawing, Editing, Dimensioning, Plotting Commands, Layering Concepts, Matching, Detailing, and Detailed Drawings
Exercises on 3D Solid Modelling: Plummer Lock, Bearings, Guideways, Generating 2D from 3D
Assembly Drawing of Mechanical Engineering Parts
Exercises on Valves, Couplings, Gears and Gear Trains, Belts, Pulleys, Modelling of Robot Grippers, Incorporating Electronic Components in CAD Models
Creating Assembly Models of Socket and Spigot Joint, Knuckle Joint, Rigid Flange Couplings, Bushed Pin Flexible Coupling
Creating Assembly Models of Plummer Block, Single Plate Clutch or Cone Friction Clutch
